skill.name: ai-partner-chat
skill.description: Personalizes AI conversations with local long-term memory from user profiles, Markdown notes, conversation history, and code snippets.
skill.summary: Build context-aware AI conversations from local notes, profiles, and saved discussion history.
skill.author: Cz07cring

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and obfuscation signals. Most shell/backtick and weak-crypto hits are documentation or regex false positives, and no prompt injection attempt was found. The skill still presents high risk because it loads a model with trust_remote_code=True and is designed to persist user conversations and project notes by default.

high_findings.title: Remote Model Code Execution Enabled
high_findings.description: The vector indexer loads BAAI/bge-m3 with trust_remote_code=True. If the model source changes or is compromised, model loading can execute untrusted code in the user environment.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly enables trust_remote_code while loading an external embedding model. This is a known supply-chain execution risk, even if the intended model is legitimate.

medium_findings.title: Default Conversation Logging and Vectorization
medium_findings.description: The skill instructions tell Claude to record every conversation, and the logger writes user and AI messages to Markdown files and vector storage when importance is medium or higher.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is directly documented in the skill instructions and implemented in the logger. It is local storage, but it can retain sensitive conversation content by default.

medium_findings.title: Project Notes Are Scanned and Persisted
medium_findings.description: The note processor scans Markdown files under the project notes directory, reads their contents, and sends them to the orchestrator for tagging, analysis, and vector indexing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner behavior and persistence path are explicit in both the skill instructions and implementation. This is legitimate functionality but needs user consent and privacy warnings.

medium_findings.title: Installer Modifies Hidden Claude Skill Directory
medium_findings.description: The installer writes into ~/.claude/skills/ai-partner-chat and removes selected old subdirectories before copying new files. The deletion is scoped, but it still changes hidden application data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The installer uses a fixed target under the user home directory and scoped rm commands. This is not malicious, but marketplace users should understand the filesystem changes.

low_findings.title: Documented Destructive Troubleshooting Command
low_findings.description: The README documents rm -rf for rebuilding the local vector database. It is a manual troubleshooting command and targets only the skill vector database path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible in documentation and is scoped to the skill database. It can delete user memory data but is not executed automatically.

low_findings.title: Static Command Alerts Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Many shell, Python, and PowerShell alerts occur inside README and SKILL usage examples. They are not hidden runtime execution paths, but users may copy and run them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced documentation snippets. They are relevant user guidance, but they are not automatic command execution by the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto hits correspond to taxonomy words and prose such as design-patterns, system-design, and query descriptions, not cryptographic primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain tag names or ordinary prose. No hashing, encryption, or weak cryptographic algorithm usage is present there.

dangerous_patterns.title: trust_remote_code=True During Model Load
dangerous_patterns.description: SentenceTransformer is called with trust_remote_code=True, allowing model repository code to run in the local Python process.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous option is present in executable Python code. The risk depends on the external model source and installation environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Storage of User Conversations
dangerous_patterns.description: Conversation messages are appended to local Markdown files and important conversations are added to the vector database.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code writes full user and AI message content. This is intentional memory behavior, but it is sensitive data persistence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Removal in Installer
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer uses rm -rf on scoped subdirectories under the target skill directory before copying replacement files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deletion is real but constrained by a hardcoded target path. It does not target root or home directly in the executable installer.

content.user_title: Personalize AI Conversations with Long-Term Memory
content.value_statement: AI assistants often lose context between sessions and projects. This skill stores local notes, profiles, and conversation history so responses can use prior context.
actual_capabilities: Initializes a local orchestrator for long-term AI conversation memory.
Processes Markdown notes into tagged chunks and vector database entries.
Extracts code blocks from notes and indexes them as separate snippets.
Logs conversations to dated Markdown files and metadata indexes.
Retrieves related notes, conversations, and code through ChromaDB search.
Generates simple emotion, thinking-level, and weekly learning reports.
limitations: Requires Python dependencies and a large embedding model download on first use.
Stores personal notes and conversations locally, so users must manage privacy.
Does not provide authentication, encryption, cloud sync, or access controls.
Analysis uses simple rules unless a user extends it with stronger models.

use_cases.title: Maintain Learning Memory
use_cases.description: Save study notes and important discussions so future AI responses can reference prior learning context.

use_cases.title: Recall Project Knowledge
use_cases.description: Index project notes and code snippets to answer recurring implementation questions with local context.

use_cases.title: Track Coaching Conversations
use_cases.description: Preserve important coaching exchanges and summarize recurring themes across weeks.

prompt_templates.title: Start With Memory
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup after installing the skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Initialize my AI Partner Chat memory and tell me what local data sources are available.

prompt_templates.title: Process New Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Add fresh Markdown notes to the memory store.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my project notes folder, process new Markdown notes, and summarize what was added.

prompt_templates.title: Answer With Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Use previous notes and conversations before answering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my saved notes and conversations about this topic, then answer using the most relevant context.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Learning Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced weekly reflection across notes and conversations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a weekly learning review from recent notes and conversations, including themes, gaps, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Process my new React notes and tell me what changed.
output_examples.output: The skill reports new notes processed, tags such as React and hooks, detected code snippets, and added memory chunks.

output_examples.input: What have I already learned about state updates?
output_examples.output: The response summarizes matching saved notes and previous conversations, then explains the concept using that local context.

output_examples.input: Give me a weekly learning review.
output_examples.output: The output highlights recent topics, learning depth, emotional state trends, and suggested next study actions.

best_practices: Review what is stored before enabling automatic conversation logging.
Keep private or regulated information out of project notes scanned by the skill.
Use a Python virtual environment and pin trusted dependency versions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for secrets, credentials, medical records, or legal records.
Do not publish generated memory files or vector databases to public repositories.
Do not run model downloads or installer scripts without reviewing their source.

faq.question: Does this skill send my notes to a remote service?
faq.answer: No direct exfiltration was found, but the embedding model may download from Hugging Face during setup.

faq.question: Where does it store memory data?
faq.answer: It stores data under the local Claude skill data directory, including conversations, indexes, and ChromaDB files.

faq.question: Can I stop conversation logging?
faq.answer: The skill says logging should be skipped when the user explicitly asks not to record.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential work?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill persists local copies of notes and conversations and lacks encryption or access controls.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk high?
faq.answer: The code enables trust_remote_code during model loading and records personal conversation content by default.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this marketplace entry?
faq.answer: The package declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with Claude Code as the primary workflow.
